title: DASH Diet Macros for Hypertension 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: DASH Diet Macro Calculator for Blood Pressure
description: 🩺 DASH diet for hypertension: 18% protein, 27% fat, 55% carbs, <2300mg sodium & 4700mg potassium. -11 mmHg in 2 weeks · Free & fast.
intro: The **DASH diet** (Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension) was developed by the NIH in 1997 to lower blood pressure without medication. Clinical trials show **-11 mmHg systolic reduction in 2 weeks**. It follows an **18% protein / 27% fat / 55% carbohydrate** distribution, emphasizing sodium reduction to under 2300 mg/day (or 1500 mg on strict DASH) and potassium intake of 4700 mg/day, magnesium 500 mg, and calcium 1250 mg. The diet prioritizes fruits (4–5 servings), vegetables (4–5), fat-free dairy (2–3), whole grains (6–8), nuts and seeds (4–5 servings/week), and limits red meat and sweets. It's ranked #1 diet by US News for over a decade.
keyTakeaway: DASH: **18% protein + 27% fat + 55% carbs**, sodium **<2300mg** (ideal 1500), potassium **>4700mg**. -11 mmHg in 2 weeks.

example.title: Example: 2000 kcal DASH
example.result: Sample day: 4 fruit + 4 vegetables + 2 fat-free dairy servings + 7 whole grain servings + 150g fish/legumes + 30g nuts/seeds.

explanation:
## What is DASH

Developed by the NIH to lower blood pressure naturally without medication.

## Macros and Micronutrients (2000 kcal)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Protein | 90 g (18%) |
| Fat | 60 g (27%) |
| Carbohydrates | 275 g (55%) |
| Sodium | <2300 mg |
| Potassium | 4700 mg |
| Calcium | 1250 mg |
| Magnesium | 500 mg |
| Fiber | 30 g |

## Daily Servings

| Food Group | Servings |
|---|---|
| Whole grains | 6–8 |
| Vegetables | 4–5 |
| Fruits | 4–5 |
| Fat-free dairy | 2–3 |
| Lean meat | ≤2 |
| Nuts/seeds | 4–5/week |

## Evidence

- **-11 mmHg systolic, -6 diastolic** in 2 weeks (DASH trial).
- **Ranked #1 diet by US News** for 14+ years.

## Foods to Limit

- Processed meats, deli meats, salty snacks.
- Fast food, canned goods, high-sodium items.
- Sugary treats, red meat.

## Common Mistakes

1. Reducing sodium without increasing potassium.
2. Choosing 'low sodium' products loaded with sugar.
3. 75% of dietary sodium is hidden in processed foods.

## Related Calculators

- [Daily sodium](/calculadora-sodio-diario-hipertension).
- [Daily potassium](/calculadora-potasio-diario-necesario).
- [Daily magnesium](/calculadora-magnesio-diario-requerido).

Consult a cardiologist before making major dietary changes.

faq.q: How much can DASH diet lower blood pressure?
faq.a: **-11 mmHg systolic and -6 diastolic** (DASH trial results).

faq.q: How much sodium should you consume on DASH?
Does DASH diet work alongside blood pressure medication?
faq.a: Yes, it enhances medication effectiveness. Consult your cardiologist.

faq.q: Can DASH diet help with weight loss?
How does DASH compare to the Mediterranean diet?
faq.a: DASH emphasizes fat-free dairy and stricter sodium control; Mediterranean focuses more on olive oil.

faq.q: How long until you see DASH diet results?
faq.a: Initial results in **14 days**; full benefits in **8 weeks**.

faq.q: What foods should you avoid on DASH diet?
faq.a: Deli meats, canned foods, sugary snacks, and red meat. Focus on whole, unprocessed foods.

faq.q: Is DASH diet safe for long-term use?
faq.a: Yes, it's endorsed by major health organizations and has decades of clinical research supporting safety.

sources.name: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
